questions.q: In which year was the Bahmani Sultanate founded?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate was established in 1347 CE as the first independent Muslim kingdom of the Deccan.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate was the first independent Muslim kingdom of which region of India?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate was the first independent Muslim state established in the Deccan plateau of southern India.

questions.q: Which city was the first capital of the Bahmani Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Gulbarga (Kalaburagi) served as the first capital of the Bahmani Sultanate before the seat was later moved to Bidar.

questions.q: To which later city did the Bahmani Sultanate move its capital?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani capital was shifted from Gulbarga to Bidar in the 15th century.

questions.q: Which powerful Hindu empire was the great southern rival of the Bahmani Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate fought repeated wars against the neighbouring Vijayanagara Empire to its south.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate eventually broke apart into how many successor states?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate fragmented into the five Deccan sultanates.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate was a kingdom of which faith's rulers?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate was a Muslim kingdom, the first independent one in the Deccan.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate arose after a revolt against which northern power?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate emerged in 1347 from a rebellion of Deccan nobles against the Delhi Sultanate of Muhammad bin Tughlaq.

questions.q: On which Indian plateau was the Bahmani Sultanate centred?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate was centred on the Deccan plateau of peninsular India.

questions.q: In which century was the Bahmani Sultanate founded?
questions.explanation: Founded in 1347, the Bahmani Sultanate was established in the 14th century CE.

questions.q: Who was the founder and first sultan of the Bahmani Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah (Zafar Khan) founded the Bahmani Sultanate in 1347 and gave it his name.

questions.q: From which Delhi sultan did the Deccan break away to form the Bahmani kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate was founded when Deccan amirs rebelled against Muhammad bin Tughlaq of Delhi.

questions.q: Which famous Persian-born minister served as a celebrated prime minister of the Bahmani Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Mahmud Gawan was the renowned Persian-born prime minister (wazir) who reformed and expanded the Bahmani state in the 15th century.

questions.q: Which madrasa, still surviving in Bidar, was built by the Bahmani minister Mahmud Gawan?
questions.explanation: Mahmud Gawan built a celebrated madrasa (college) at Bidar, an important centre of learning whose ruins still stand.

questions.q: The river that often formed a contested frontier between the Bahmanis and Vijayanagara was the:
questions.explanation: The fertile Raichur doab between the Krishna and Tungabhadra rivers was repeatedly fought over by the Bahmanis and Vijayanagara.

questions.q: The Gulbarga Fort, an early Bahmani stronghold, contained a notable mosque inspired by which architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: The Jama Masjid within Gulbarga Fort is famous for a covered courtyard design said to echo the great mosque of Cordoba in Spain.

questions.q: Which of these was one of the five Deccan sultanates into which the Bahmani kingdom split?
questions.explanation: Bijapur under the Adil Shahi dynasty was one of the five Deccan sultanates that emerged from the Bahmani breakup.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi sultanate of Golconda, which broke away from the Bahmanis, was centred near which modern city?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi dynasty ruled from Golconda, a fort near present-day Hyderabad.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate is noted for blending Persian culture with which regional Deccani element?
options: The Deccani Urdu language and local traditions
questions.explanation: Bahmani patronage helped foster Deccani culture, including the early development of the Deccani form of Urdu, blending Persian and local influences.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the Bahmani Sultanate endure before fully fragmenting?
questions.explanation: Founded in 1347 and effectively dissolved by the early 16th century, the Bahmani Sultanate lasted roughly 170 years.

questions.q: The Bahmani Sultanate drew many of its officials and soldiers from two competing factions known as the:
questions.explanation: Bahmani politics was shaped by rivalry between the Deccani (local-born) nobles and the Afaqi or Pardesi (foreign-born, often Persian) nobles.

questions.q: The Bahmani sultans encouraged the immigration of scholars and soldiers from which region to staff their court?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani court actively recruited talented Afaqis from Persia, Arabia, and Central Asia, giving it a strong Persianate character.

questions.q: What was the original name and title of Hasan, the founder of the Bahmani dynasty, before he became sultan?
questions.explanation: The founder rose as the noble Zafar Khan before assuming the throne as Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah in 1347.

questions.q: Under which Bahmani sultan did the minister Mahmud Gawan rise to his greatest influence?
questions.explanation: Mahmud Gawan's power peaked as the chief minister of Muhammad Shah III (Lashkari) in the later 15th century.

questions.q: Mahmud Gawan's career ended when he was executed in 1481 after being:
options: Falsely accused of treason by court rivals
Killed in a battle with Vijayanagara
questions.explanation: Mahmud Gawan was executed in 1481 on the basis of a forged letter and false treason charges engineered by jealous Deccani nobles, a blow that hastened the kingdom's decline.

questions.q: To improve administration, Mahmud Gawan reorganised the Bahmani kingdom into provinces called:
questions.explanation: Mahmud Gawan restructured the realm into provinces known as tarafs to curb the power of provincial governors.

questions.q: Which Bahmani sultan moved the capital from Gulbarga to Bidar in the early 15th century?
questions.explanation: Ahmad Shah I (Ahmad Shah Wali) shifted the Bahmani capital from Gulbarga to Bidar around 1424-1425.

questions.q: Besides Bijapur and Golconda, which of these was another of the five splinter Deccan sultanates?
questions.explanation: Ahmadnagar under the Nizam Shahi dynasty was one of the five successor states, alongside Bijapur, Golconda, Bidar (Barid Shahi), and Berar (Imad Shahi).

questions.q: The tombs of the early Bahmani sultans are clustered in a royal necropolis near which city?
questions.explanation: The Haft Gumbaz at Gulbarga is a complex of domed tombs of early Bahmani rulers.

questions.q: The five Deccan sultanates that succeeded the Bahmanis later allied to defeat Vijayanagara at which 1565 battle?
questions.explanation: Several of the Bahmani successor sultanates combined to crush Vijayanagara at the Battle of Talikota in 1565.
